title: About Boys Who Cook
subtitle: Learn about our story, mission, and the team behind our movement.

story.heading: Our Story
story.text:
Boys Who Cook is built on a simple but powerful idea: teaching boys to cook matters. While cooking is often seen as just a basic life skill and catered to one gender, I see it as a way to build confidence, independence, and character.

Our mission is to empower young boys by challenging gender stereotypes around cooking and life skills, building a movement that creates confidence, independence, and equality for the next generation through cooking. Our vision is to empower young boys all over the world to build life skills such as confidence and independence through the art of cooking.

Through cooking, boys learn patience, creativity, teamwork, and self-reliance. The kitchen becomes a space to grow, take risks, and feel proud of what they create.

Boys Who Cook was created to fill a gap in how boys are encouraged to develop these skills and to make sure every boy has the opportunity to discover what they’re capable of.

mission_vision.mission_text: To empower boys through culinary education by providing hands-on cooking experiences that build confidence, life skills, and community.
mission_vision.vision_text: To empower young boys all over the world to to build life skills such as confidence and independence through the art of cooking.

values.title: Empowerment
values.description: We believe in the power of every young man to grow, lead, and make a difference through the skills they develop in the kitchen.

values.title: Community
values.description: Cooking brings people together. We build strong, supportive communities where boys can learn, share, and thrive.

values.title: Creativity
values.description: The kitchen is a place for creativity and self-expression. We encourage every participant to explore new flavors and ideas.

values.title: Service
values.description: We teach boys not just to cook for themselves, but to use their skills to serve and give back to their communities.
